name: Lactococcus lactis (MG1363)
shortDescription: Food-grade, GRAS lactic acid bacterium used for centuries in dairy fermentation. Non-colonizing and ideal for ingestible / clinical biosensors where a food-safe, gut-transient host is wanted.

safety.grasBasis: FDA GRAS for food use; EFSA QPS; centuries of safe use in dairy.
safety.humanUse: Food fermentation; engineered L. lactis trialed clinically (e.g. mucosal delivery).
safety.biocontainment: thyA-based biological containment demonstrated in clinical trials (transient survival).
safety.bslSource: ABSA Risk Group 1; FDA GRAS; EFSA QPS.
safety.notes: Gram-positive, no LPS endotoxin; transient gut passage (does not colonize).

engineering.crisprTools: dCas9/CRISPRi demonstrated in lactic acid bacteria.

provenance.source: NCBI Taxonomy; FDA GRAS / EFSA QPS
